key_insight: The paper unifies stochastic microscale gliding dynamics, nonlocal Keller--Segel analysis, and physics-informed neural networks to model myxobacterial aggregation on rough bounded domains.
engineering_relevance: It provides a mathematically grounded simulation framework for predicting collective bacterial motion in realistic geometries where classical smooth-surface models fail.
method: Discrete agent model + subordinated Langevin dynamics with an inverse stable subordinator
Derivation of a time-nonlocal Keller--Segel system with logarithmic sensitivity
Mesh-free, positivity-preserving, multi-objective PINN for time-marching simulation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports rigorous theoretical results and numerical experiments on complex geometries, including a butterfly-shaped domain, to demonstrate robustness and accuracy.
watch_item: The method’s stability and fidelity may depend on careful handling of singular logarithmic sensitivity, memory effects, and positivity constraints in the PINN implementation.
paper_title: Dynamics of Chemotactic Gliding-Aggregation in Myxobacteria on Bounded Domains: Stochastic Modeling, Analysis, and Deep Neural Network Simulations
